label: Base Shell
role: Foundation — low-wide shrine base (80×60×30mm) with internal cam cavity and 170g weight chamber.
print_risk: Large flat surface (80×60mm) prone to warping. Use 8mm brim + 60°C bed + enclosed chamber.
artifact_role: Rooted presence. The wide low base creates a 'shrine' feeling — the archivist is not standing on the desk, it grows from it.
motion_note: The base is anchored to the desk. It does not move. Its stillness is the foundation for all other motion.

label: Torso Shell
role: Lean body — 24×24×50mm shell with internal follower guide and side maintenance panel slot. Forward lean 10°.
print_risk: Internal cavity support difficult to remove. Use tree support at 10% density. Access via side panel slot.
artifact_role: The reading posture. The 10° forward lean creates focus and attention. The slim 24mm width makes it elegant, not bulky.
motion_note: The torso receives the breath through the follower, but its movement is subtle — 25% of the follower displacement. It is the body that breathes, not the mechanism that drives.

label: Head Shell
role: Faceless ellipsoid — Ø20×25mm. No eyes, mouth, or nose. The blankness invites projection.
print_risk: Ø4.5mm neck hole requires support inside. Remove carefully and ream with shaft scrap.
artifact_role: The soul of the archivist. The absence of a face is not a missing feature — it is the feature. Gestalt psychology makes the viewer project emotion onto the blank surface.
motion_note: The head moves least — only 12% of the displacement — because the neck is thin and the head is delicate. Its tiny motion is what makes the breath feel deep rather than shallow.

label: Neck Sleeve
role: Neck pivot — Ø6×12mm sleeve with flanges. Connects head to torso. Internal bore Ø4.3mm for stainless shaft.
print_risk: Thin wall (1mm). Use 0.12mm layer height for precision. May need reaming.
artifact_role: The gesture joint. The visible neck creates elegance and separates head from body. The thin Ø4mm shaft implies fragility and care.
motion_note: The neck acts as a flexible connector between torso and head. It inherits the body's motion but with slight lag, creating a natural chain of movement.

label: Partition
role: Divider plate — separates cam cavity (upper) from weight chamber (lower). M3 screw mount.
print_risk: Screw holes may misalign due to shrinkage. Design Ø4mm holes for M3 tolerance.
artifact_role: Hidden order. The partition is never seen, but without it the mechanism and weight would mix. It represents the invisible structure behind presence.
motion_note: Fixed to the base. It does not move.

label: Access Panel
role: Removable bottom cover — provides access to weight chamber for adjustment. M3 screws + rubber pad slot.
print_risk: Flat plate may warp. Print with brim.
artifact_role: Maintenance as humility. The panel admits that the artifact needs care. It is not sealed like a consumer product — it invites intervention.
motion_note: Fixed to the base. It does not move.

label: Breathing Cam
role: Eccentric disc cam — core of the breathing mechanism. Ø25mm total, 5mm lift, 5mm thickness.
material_hint: PETG, red or dark grey, 30% fill
print_risk: Profile precision critical. Use 0.12mm layer height. Polish circumference with 600-grit after printing.
artifact_role: The hidden heart. Inside the base, this small disc converts rotation into life. Its eccentric design is not mechanical — it is the rhythm of breath made solid.
motion_note: The cam rotates on the shaft. Its eccentricity creates the rise and fall that the follower translates into breath. One full rotation = one breath cycle.

label: Follower
role: Flat-bottom follower + guide slider. Ø12mm base, 20mm guide height. Converts cam lift to torso motion.
print_risk: Bottom surface must be absolutely flat — any bump causes stutter. Print vertically with tree support. Polish bottom face.
artifact_role: The translator. The follower speaks the language of the cam and turns it into the body's breath. It is the joint between mechanism and organism.
motion_note: The follower moves vertically, following the cam's eccentric profile. Its displacement is 100% — the full amplitude of the breath. It is the most visible motion in the mechanism.

label: Crank
role: L-shaped hand crank with spherical grip. 30mm arm length. Human-scale input for the breathing ritual.
print_risk: Spherical grip overhangs >45°. Use tree support at 15% density. Polish grip for hand feel.
artifact_role: The invitation. The crank is the only place the human touches. Its worn polished surface will record every ritual. It says: 'You are welcome to participate.'
motion_note: The crank rotates with the shaft, but visually it orbits around the base exterior. Its rotation speed is the breath frequency — slow, deliberate, human-scale.
